title: Cloudflare Just Built a WordPress Killer

content.text: If you're using Claude Code right now,
two things happened this week that
affects you directly. First, Anthropic
leaked Claude Code's entire source code.
That's half a million lines of code, all
the unreleased features, system prompts,
everything. We'll go through what was
revealed and what that means for
business owners and marketers. Second,
usage limits are burning faster than
ever. We've got some practical tips to
stretch your tokens further. And
Cloudflare launched Mdash, an
open-source WordPress alternative built
on Astro, the same framework we vibe
coded our website on. I'm Mark Webster.
I'm joined as always by my co-host and
co-founder of Authority Hacker, Gail
Brandon. Gail, it was a big leak. I
think I read the number 512,000 lines of
code 1,96
files. Basically, the entire source code
of Claude Code was leaked. Like, how did
this happen? Did they just vibe code it
and like was that some kind of mistake?
>> So that's what everyone was saying on X,
but the guy who created code C code like
Boris just said that it was a human
error. Actually, they just [ __ ] it up.
It happens, you know. It's just it's not
great, but it happened. And yeah, that's
a big big blow for Entropic who like
historically has been one of the only
developers of these kind of harnesses to
keep it closed uh code like before like
almost everyone else is open source. So
for example, Codex from OpenAI is open
source. you have access to the code for
a long time. But CL code was trying to
build a bit of a mode by keeping things
secret and there's no more secrets.
>> And I think it's fair to say they did
have a decent mode. They had a very
loyal following of users. Like even when
you know you could argue that codeex
openi's model was getting better for
certain use cases like like coding still
a lot of people just were like nah I
think cloud's good enough for what we

in switching. I like the features. I
like how it works. and you know that was
fine but now it's kind of like opened
the floodgates to everyone kind of
ripping off everything they do basically
and they may I don't know do you think
they may even have to make claude code
open source completely or
>> nah I think they will just start over
like the point is like yeah now it's
like you can give this code base to your
AI agent and be like hey build the same
feature on my AI agent and then it's
like it's going to take you know half an
hour and you get a workable version like
you still need to refine it if you're a
big company and so on but the point is
it's that simple You have the whole
blueprint. You have every single system

behind the scenes and it's like the

which means in the near future probably
all competitors will be able to rip off
all the best features of cloud code and

their code which means cloud has to

that's kind of a blow to a company that
has been probably doing the best job in
the industry doing this. But at the same
time, if you consider the rate at which
Entropic is shipping features, which is
pretty much every day there's something
new in code, it will only take a few new
features after that leak to come out for
them to start rebuilding a mode and
people wanting to stick to it. On top of
the fact that even with recent rate
limits, you still get an incredible deal
in how much usage you get for how much
you spend with a subscription.
>> No, we have we do actually have some
insights into some of those new features
because they were also released in
>> Yeah, because they have betas, right?
>> Uh let's talk about some of those now. I

>> I guess it's like an always on

their memories. Actually, your memories
get consolidated inside your brain while
you sleep, which is why they call it
auto sleep because they mimicked the
logic of that, which is when you're not
using it, the model kind of like goes
back through your chat history and
things that were done, etc. And
essentially makes a summary of what
happens and keeps like a much shorter
version of that inside its memory files
to get a little bit of continuity
between chats and the ability to work on
the same codebase, file system, etc. But
again like yeah when your memor is
considered in your brain like you don't
remember every single detail. They kind

[laughter] brain as well. And so they
they've just mimic that. There was some

allow like a feeling of continuity when
you walk inside the project on code.
>> Yeah. Apparently it's going to write

that day, everything you've said. Yeah.
So it's I guess you're right like is
